one_time_description: Robinson Huron Treaty Litigation Fund settlement - past compensation from Federal and Ontario Provincial Government for breached obligations under the Robinson Huron Treaty of 1850
economic_notes: First Nation has 51% interest in Helios Whitefish River Limited Partnership; 16.55% interest in Mnidoo Mnising Power Limited Partnership; participates in Great Lakes Hospitality Group Limited Partnership (hotel and conference centre on Manitoulin Island with 6 other First Nations); fully consolidates Whitefish River Development Corporation and Birch Island Construction Company
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to non-adoption of PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations (effective April 1, 2022) - management was unable to obtain reasonable assessment of tangible capital assets for potential asset retirement obligations, making it impossible to quantify the impact on expenses, annual surplus, tangible capital assets, asset retirement obligation, and accumulated surplus for 2024 and 2023. The dominant item is the Robinson Huron Treaty settlement of $377,600,777 recognized as revenue in 2024, with a receivable of $377,563,108 recorded. Funds will be paid in tranches over time; subsequent to year-end, First Nation committed to per capita distribution to members with balance going to a trust. The First Nation also continued to follow PS 3270 Solid Waste Landfill Closure and Post-Closure Liability. Two entities fully consolidated: Whitefish River Development Corporation and Birch Island Construction Company. Cash flow statement shows repayment of long-term debt as positive $477,425 which appears to reflect net change. osr calculation includes Robinson Huron Treaty settlement and interest/other revenue as own-source; total_government_transfers includes ISC $11,789,881 + CMHC $419,032 + Ontario $2,027,387 + Anishinabek organizations $5,722,146 + OFNLP $1,134,252 = $21,092,698 however OFNLP and Anishinabek may be partially non-government; expenses_administration includes Lands and Economic Development $1,642,526 + Administration $3,046,966 = $4,689,492.
